wiki_catalogs.name: Project Overview
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for the Payroll project overview section focused on the Indonesian payroll and HRIS system. Explain its purpose as a comprehensive backend solution built with FastAPI and SQLAlchemy, its core functionality including automated payroll processing, employee management, and tax compliance with Indonesian regulations. Document the system architecture, key technologies (FastAPI, SQLAlchemy, Alembic), and target audience. Include both conceptual overviews for beginners and technical details for experienced developers. Use terminology consistent with the codebase. Provide practical examples demonstrating common use cases like payslip generation, attendance tracking, and tax computation. Document the multi-tenant architecture and regulatory compliance features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Getting Started
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive getting started content for the Payroll system. Cover prerequisites including Python 3.x knowledge, SQL fundamentals, and Indonesian payroll regulations understanding. Document installation procedures step-by-step, environment setup with virtual environments, dependency installation using requirements.txt, and initial database configuration. Include first-run instructions for setting up the database with Alembic migrations, configuring environment variables, and running the development server. Provide practical examples of accessing the API endpoints and initial system configuration. Address common setup issues and their solutions.

wiki_catalogs.name: Authentication & Authorization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the authentication and authorization system. Thoroughly explain the user management implementation, role-based access control (RBAC) system, permission mechanisms, and company isolation features. Document the authentication flow, token-based security, password hashing with bcrypt, and multi-tenant security boundaries. Include concrete examples from the auth.py model showing user creation, role assignment, and permission checking. Document the relationship between users, companies, and their access permissions. Address security considerations, session management, and integration with FastAPI dependencies.

wiki_catalogs.name: Employee Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the employee management system. Thoroughly explain employee record management, organizational structure implementation, department and position configurations, and employment status tracking. Document the employee model relationships, hierarchical organization structure, and employee data validation rules. Include concrete examples showing employee creation, position assignment, department mapping, and organizational chart maintenance. Explain the integration with salary structures, attendance tracking, and payroll processing. Address employee lifecycle management and data integrity constraints.

wiki_catalogs.name: Salary & Compensation
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the salary and compensation system. Thoroughly explain the salary grade system, grade salary matrix, allowance types and management, deduction configurations, and employee-specific compensation setups. Document the salary structure models, allowance formulas, and dynamic compensation calculation methods. Include concrete examples showing salary grade creation, allowance configuration, employee allowance assignments, and deduction management. Explain the relationship between salary grades, individual employee salaries, and payroll calculations. Address compensation policies and regulatory compliance aspects.

wiki_catalogs.name: Payroll Processing
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the payroll processing system. Thoroughly explain payroll run configuration, payslip generation, batch processing workflows, and approval processes. Document the payroll calculation algorithms, payslip line item processing, and automated payment generation. Include concrete examples showing payroll run creation, payslip generation, batch processing execution, and payslip distribution. Explain the integration with attendance records, salary structures, tax calculations, and BPJS contributions. Address payroll scheduling, approval workflows, and compliance reporting features.

wiki_catalogs.name: Tax Compliance
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the tax compliance system focusing on Indonesian tax regulations. Thoroughly explain PPh Pasal 17 calculation methods, PTKP (Personal Tax Deduction) management, tax bracket configuration, and tax setting administration. Document the tax calculation algorithms, progressive tax computation, and regulatory compliance features. Include concrete examples showing tax setting updates, PTKP configuration, bracket adjustments, and tax computation results. Explain the integration with payroll processing, employee tax obligations, and monthly tax reporting. Address tax law compliance and regulatory updates.

wiki_catalogs.name: BPJS Integration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the BPJS (social security) integration system. Thoroughly explain BPJS settings management, contribution calculation methods, social security contribution administration, and employee benefit tracking. Document the BPJS contribution algorithms, employer and employee contribution splits, and regulatory compliance features. Include concrete examples showing BPJS setting configuration, contribution calculation, benefit enrollment, and contribution deduction processing. Explain the integration with payroll processing, tax calculations, and employee benefit management. Address social security regulations and contribution reporting.

wiki_catalogs.name: Attendance & Overtime
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the attendance and overtime management system. Thoroughly explain attendance tracking, overtime calculation methods, shift configuration, work hour computation, and attendance validation rules. Document the attendance recording system, overtime policy enforcement, shift scheduling, and work hour accumulation. Include concrete examples showing attendance entry, overtime calculation, shift configuration, and attendance reporting. Explain the integration with payroll processing, leave management, and productivity tracking. Address attendance policies and timekeeping regulations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Leave Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the leave management system. Thoroughly explain leave types, leave balance management, leave request processing, leave policies, and leave impact on payroll. Document the leave allocation system, leave request workflows, approval processes, and leave balance calculations. Include concrete examples showing leave type configuration, leave request submission, approval workflows, and leave balance tracking. Explain the integration with attendance records, payroll processing, and employee lifecycle management. Address leave policies and regulatory compliance.

wiki_catalogs.name: Bonus & Reimbursement
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the bonus and reimbursement management system. Thoroughly explain bonus types, bonus calculation methods, bonus disbursement processing, reimbursement types, and reimbursement claim management. Document the bonus configuration system, bonus formula implementation, bonus approval workflows, and reimbursement processing pipelines. Include concrete examples showing bonus type setup, bonus calculation, bonus distribution, reimbursement claims, and reimbursement processing. Explain the integration with payroll processing and financial accounting systems.

wiki_catalogs.name: Kasbon Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the kasbon (employee advance) management system. Thoroughly explain kasbon request processing, installment management, employee advance tracking, and kasbon repayment schedules. Document the kasbon application workflow, approval processes, installment calculation methods, and repayment tracking. Include concrete examples showing kasbon request submission, approval workflows, installment planning, and repayment management. Explain the integration with payroll processing and employee account management. Address kasbon policies and financial controls.

wiki_catalogs.name: Field Visit Management
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the field visit management system. Thoroughly explain field visit tracking, travel allowance processing, visit scheduling, and expense management. Document the field visit recording system, travel allowance calculation methods, visit approval workflows, and expense reporting. Include concrete examples showing field visit registration, travel allowance calculation, visit scheduling, and expense processing. Explain the integration with attendance tracking, payroll processing, and expense accounting. Address travel policies and expense regulations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Integration & Localization
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the integration and localization system. Thoroughly explain multi-language support (Indonesian/English), AI integration with LangChain and OpenAI, external system integrations, and translation management. Document the internationalization framework, language switching mechanisms, AI-powered features, and API integration capabilities. Include concrete examples showing language configuration, AI feature usage, external system connections, and localization setup. Explain the integration patterns and extensibility for custom integrations.

wiki_catalogs.name: Database Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive content for database configuration and management. Explain the database setup process, connection management, Alembic migration system, and schema versioning. Document the SQLAlchemy configuration, database engine setup, session management, and connection pooling. Include concrete examples showing database initialization, migration execution, schema updates, and database maintenance. Explain the multi-tenant database architecture, company isolation, and data partitioning strategies. Address database performance optimization and backup procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Data Seeding & Configuration
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Develop detailed content for the data seeding and configuration system. Thoroughly explain the seed data setup process, Indonesian regulation configuration, default system settings, and initial data population. Document the seed data structure, regulatory compliance setup, default configuration templates, and data initialization procedures. Include concrete examples showing seed data execution, regulatory configuration, default setup, and data validation. Explain the relationship between seed data and system functionality, and how to customize default configurations for different organizations.

wiki_catalogs.name: API Reference
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create API documentation for the Payroll system RESTful endpoints. Document HTTP methods, URL patterns, request/response schemas, and authentication requirements for all major API groups including authentication, employee management, payroll processing, attendance tracking, and tax management. Include endpoint-specific examples, parameter descriptions, response formats, error handling strategies, and authentication methods. Document common use cases, client implementation guidelines, and API versioning information. Address rate limiting, security considerations, and API testing procedures.

wiki_catalogs.name: Development Guidelines
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive development guidelines for contributing to the Payroll system. Document code standards, testing strategies, development environment setup, and contribution procedures. Explain the project structure, coding conventions, database development practices, and API development standards. Include guidelines for adding new features, extending existing functionality, maintaining backward compatibility, and following the established architectural patterns. Address code review processes, testing requirements, and documentation standards for contributors.

wiki_catalogs.name: Deployment & Operations
wiki_catalogs.prompt: Create comprehensive deployment and operations documentation for the Payroll system. Document production setup procedures, environment configuration, database deployment, and system maintenance. Explain containerization options, deployment strategies, monitoring setup, logging configuration, and performance optimization. Include procedures for system updates, backup and recovery, security hardening, and operational troubleshooting. Address scaling considerations, disaster recovery, and maintenance procedures for production environments.

wiki_overview.content:
<blog>
# Comprehensive Project Analysis: Indonesian Payroll & HRIS System

## 1. Project Introduction

### Purpose Statement
The Indonesian Payroll & HRIS (Human Resource Information System) is a comprehensive web-based payroll management solution designed specifically for Indonesian companies. The system provides end-to-end functionality for employee management, salary calculations, tax compliance with Indonesian regulations, and automated payroll processing.

### Core Goals and Objectives
- **Regulatory Compliance**: Implement Indonesian tax laws (PPh Pasal 17, PTKP values) and BPJS (social security) contributions
- **Automated Payroll Processing**: Streamline salary calculations, allowances, deductions, and tax computations
- **HR Management**: Centralized employee data management with organizational structure support
- **Multi-Company Support**: Scalable architecture supporting multiple organizations within a single instance
- **Audit Trail**: Complete tracking of all payroll operations and modifications

### Target Audience
- Indonesian companies requiring automated payroll processing
- Human Resources departments managing multiple locations
- Accounting teams handling complex tax calculations
- Small to medium enterprises seeking professional payroll solutions

## 2. Technical Architecture

### Component Breakdown
The system follows a modular FastAPI architecture with SQLAlchemy ORM for data persistence:

```mermaid
graph TD
    A[FastAPI Application] --> B[Database Layer]
    A --> C[Authentication & Authorization]
    A --> D[Payroll Processing Engine]
    A --> E[HR Management]
    
    B --> F[SQLAlchemy Models]
    B --> G[Alembic Migrations]
    
    C --> H[RBAC System]
    C --> I[User Management]
    
    D --> J[Salary Calculation]
    D --> K[Tax Computation]
    D --> L[BPJS Contributions]
    
    E --> M[Employee Records]
    E --> N[Department Structure]
    E --> O[Leave Management]
```

### Design Patterns
- **Repository Pattern**: Database operations abstracted through SQLAlchemy models
- **Factory Pattern**: Dynamic model creation for different company configurations
- **Strategy Pattern**: Pluggable tax calculation methods (PASAL_17 vs TER)
- **Observer Pattern**: Audit trail logging for all data modifications

### System Relationships
The architecture emphasizes separation of concerns with clear boundaries between HR management, payroll processing, and compliance modules.

## 3. Key Implementation

### Main Entry Points
- **Database Configuration**: `app/database.py` - SQLite engine setup with foreign key enforcement
- **Model Registry**: `app/models/__init__.py` - Centralized model imports and exports
- **Migration Environment**: `alembic/env.py` - Alembic configuration for database migrations

### Core Modules
- **Authentication & Authorization**: `app/models/auth.py` - RBAC system with granular permissions
- **Employee Management**: `app/models/employee.py` - Comprehensive employee data structures
- **Payroll Processing**: `app/models/payroll.py` - Payslip generation and payroll runs
- **Salary Structures**: `app/models/salary.py` - Grade systems and allowance management
- **Tax Compliance**: `app/models/tax.py` - Indonesian tax calculation models
- **BPJS Integration**: `app/models/bpjs.py` - Social security contribution management

### Configuration Approach
- **Environment Variables**: Database URL configuration via `DATABASE_URL`
- **Alembic Migrations**: `alembic.ini` - Database version control and schema management
- **Seed Data**: `app/seed/seed_data.py` - Automated initialization with Indonesian regulatory data

### External Dependencies
Key dependencies include:
- **FastAPI**: Web framework for REST API development
- **SQLAlchemy**: ORM for database operations
- **Alembic**: Database migration tool
- **Pydantic**: Data validation and serialization
- **LangChain/OpenAI**: AI-powered features for enhanced functionality

### Integration Points
- **Indonesian Regulatory Compliance**: Direct implementation of PPh Pasal 17, PTKP values, and BPJS rates
- **Multi-Language Support**: Built-in localization with Indonesian and English
- **Audit Trail Integration**: Comprehensive logging for all payroll operations

### Component Relationship Diagrams

```mermaid
graph LR
    A[Company] --> B[Employees]
    A --> C[Departments]
    A --> D[Payroll Settings]
    
    B --> E[Salary Grades]
    B --> F[Allowances]
    B --> G[Deductions]
    
    D --> H[Tax Settings]
    D --> I[BPJS Settings]
    D --> J[Overtime Settings]
    
    K[Payroll Run] --> L[Payslips]
    L --> M[Payslip Lines]
    
    N[Attendance] --> O[Leave Management]
    N --> P[Overtime Records]
```

## 4. Key Features

### Functionality Overview
The system provides comprehensive payroll and HR management with Indonesian-specific compliance:

#### Core Payroll Features
- **Automated Salary Calculations**: Base salary, allowances, overtime, bonuses
- **Tax Computation**: PPh Pasal 17 progressive tax calculation with PTKP deductions
- **BPJS Contributions**: Automatic social security deductions (Kesehatan, JHT, JP, JKK, JKM)
- **Payslip Generation**: Detailed payslips with itemized breakdowns
- **Batch Processing**: Multi-employee payroll runs with approval workflows

#### HR Management Features
- **Employee Records**: Complete personal and employment data
- **Organizational Structure**: Hierarchical department management
- **Leave Management**: Annual, sick, maternity, paternity, and personal leave tracking
- **Attendance Tracking**: Work hours, overtime, tardiness monitoring
- **Performance Integration**: Bonus and reimbursement management

#### Compliance Features
- **Regulatory Updates**: Automatic compliance with latest Indonesian tax regulations
- **Audit Trails**: Complete transaction history for all payroll operations
- **Multi-Currency Support**: Indonesian Rupiah (IDR) with configurable exchange rates
- **Report Generation**: Comprehensive payroll and HR reporting capabilities

### Implementation Highlights

#### Database Schema Architecture
```mermaid
erDiagram
    COMPANY ||--o{ DEPARTMENTS : has
    COMPANY ||--o{ POSITIONS : has
    COMPANY ||--o{ EMPLOYEES : has
    COMPANY ||--o{ GRADES : has
    
    DEPARTMENTS ||--o{ EMPLOYEES : contains
    POSITIONS ||--o{ EMPLOYEES : defines
    GRADES ||--o{ EMPLOYEES : assigned_to
    
    EMPLOYEES ||--o{ SALARY_MATRIX : defines
    EMPLOYEES ||--o{ ALLOWANCES : receives
    EMPLOYEES ||--o{ DEDUCTIONS : subject_to
    
    PAYROLL_RUN ||--o{ PAYSLIPS : generates
    PAYSLIPS ||--o{ PAYSLIP_LINES : contains
```

#### Tax Calculation Flow
```mermaid
stateDiagram-v2
    [*] --> EmployeeData
    EmployeeData --> SalaryCalculation
    SalaryCalculation --> AllowanceCalculation
    AllowanceCalculation --> TaxCalculation
    TaxCalculation --> BPJSCalculation
    BPJSCalculation --> NetSalaryCalculation
    NetSalaryCalculation --> [*]
    
    EmployeeData --> ValidatePTKP
    ValidatePTKP --> TaxCalculation
    
    SalaryCalculation --> ValidateGrades
    ValidateGrades --> AllowanceCalculation
```

### Feature Architecture Diagrams

#### Multi-Tenant Architecture
```mermaid
graph TD
    A[Main Database] --> B[Company 1]
    A --> C[Company 2]
    A --> D[Company N]
    
    B --> E[Company 1 Data]
    C --> F[Company 2 Data]
    D --> G[Company N Data]
    
    E --> H[Isolated Payroll Runs]
    F --> I[Isolated Employee Records]
    G --> J[Isolated Configurations]
```

## 5. Conclusion and Recommendations

### Project Architecture Summary
The Indonesian Payroll & HRIS system demonstrates a well-architected, modular approach to payroll management with strong emphasis on regulatory compliance and scalability. The system successfully integrates Indonesian labor laws, tax regulations, and social security requirements into a cohesive platform.

### Key Architectural Strengths
- **Comprehensive Compliance**: Direct implementation of Indonesian tax and social security regulations
- **Scalable Design**: Multi-tenant architecture supporting multiple organizations
- **Modular Structure**: Clear separation between HR management, payroll processing, and compliance modules
- **Future-Ready**: Extensible foundation for AI-powered features and advanced analytics

### Potential Improvement Areas
- **API Documentation**: Enhanced OpenAPI documentation for better developer experience
- **Testing Coverage**: Expanded unit and integration testing suite
- **Performance Optimization**: Index optimization and query performance tuning
- **Security Hardening**: Additional authentication and authorization layers

### Actionable Recommendations
1. **Implement Comprehensive Testing**: Add pytest coverage for all business logic modules
2. **Enhance Monitoring**: Integrate logging and metrics collection for production monitoring
3. **API Versioning**: Establish API versioning strategy for backward compatibility
4. **Documentation**: Create detailed technical documentation for deployment and maintenance

### Next Steps for Project Evolution
- **AI Integration**: Leverage LangChain and OpenAI for intelligent payroll insights
- **Mobile Interface**: Develop mobile-responsive frontend for field workers
- **Advanced Analytics**: Implement payroll trend analysis and forecasting
- **Integration APIs**: Provide third-party integrations with accounting software

**Sources:**
- [requirements.txt](requirements.txt)
- [database.py](app/database.py)
- [base.py](app/models/base.py)
- [employee.py](app/models/employee.py)
- [payroll.py](app/models/payroll.py)
- [salary.py](app/models/salary.py)
- [auth.py](app/models/auth.py)
- [tax.py](app/models/tax.py)
- [bpjs.py](app/models/bpjs.py)
- [seed_data.py](app/seed/seed_data.py)
- [env.py](alembic/env.py)
- [alembic.ini](alembic.ini)
- [models/__init__.py](app/models/__init__.py)

</blog>
